verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a TM domain or GPI anchor, localized intra-acrosomally in sperm but also expressed in other tissues (cytoplasmic). It forms a stable complex with IZUMO3 (a TM family member) on sperm. After the acrosome reaction, acrosomal contents are exposed/released on the sperm head; anti-IZUMO4 antibodies block sperm–oocyte interaction assays and IZUMO4–JUNO binding, demonstrating functional accessibility on intact gametes. Surface exposure is restricted to the germline (sperm) lineage following acrosome reaction, fitting tissue/lineage-restricted display.
